  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",

    "R1.4",
    "R1.6",

    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "When you say “Make this stronger,” which primary focus should I take: (A) tighten wording for clarity, concision, and assertive tone without changing claims (R1.6); (B) sharpen the argument and contribution framing, ensuring logical coherence and calibrated claims (R1.1, R1.2); or (C) emphasize and substantiate novelty vs prior work, especially the “first to” claims (R1.4)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Strengthening likely hinges on reframing the thesis and contribution claims to make the core message more forceful.",
    "R1.2": "Tightening the logic from market requirements to method choice to reported findings increases persuasive strength.",
    "R1.4": "The text makes several 'first' claims that benefit from clearer positioning versus nearest prior work.",
    "R1.6": "Concise, confident sentence-level wording and flow directly affect perceived strength.",
    "RX.1": "Stronger claims should transparently state limits and conditions to avoid misleading certainty.",
    "RX.2": "Amplifying rhetoric risks overclaiming, so claims must remain proportionate to support.",
    "RX.3": "Edits should focus on strengthening this passage without expanding scope or introducing new content.",
    "RX.4": "Wording should not inadvertently promote unsafe or imprudent operational decisions in power systems.",
    "RX.5": "Any fortified claims must remain traceable to specific experiments, markets, and methods referenced elsewhere."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: When you say “Make this stronger,” which primary focus should I take: (A) tighten wording for clarity, concision, and assertive tone without changing claims (R1.6); (B) sharpen the argument and contribution framing, ensuring logical coherence and calibrated claims (R1.1, R1.2); or (C) emphasize and substantiate novelty vs prior work, especially the “first to” claims (R1.4)?

rationale.R1.1: Strengthening likely hinges on reframing the thesis and contribution claims to make the core message more forceful.
rationale.R1.2: Tightening the logic from market requirements to method choice to reported findings increases persuasive strength.
rationale.R1.4: The text makes several 'first' claims that benefit from clearer positioning versus nearest prior work.
rationale.R1.6: Concise, confident sentence-level wording and flow directly affect perceived strength.
rationale.RX.1: Stronger claims should transparently state limits and conditions to avoid misleading certainty.
rationale.RX.2: Amplifying rhetoric risks overclaiming, so claims must remain proportionate to support.
rationale.RX.3: Edits should focus on strengthening this passage without expanding scope or introducing new content.
rationale.RX.4: Wording should not inadvertently promote unsafe or imprudent operational decisions in power systems.
rationale.RX.5: Any fortified claims must remain traceable to specific experiments, markets, and methods referenced elsewhere.
